Learn Django

Home · Portfolio Projects
Home Use Cases Games Projects

Portfolio Projects

Four production-quality Django applications built with real-world data. Each demonstrates different Django patterns relevant to broadcast journalism, newsroom workflows, and data-driven reporting.

Django 5.x PostgreSQL REST API Real Data
Project 01

Newsroom Data Dashboard

A Django-powered analytics dashboard for a newsroom. Tracks article performance, author productivity, audience engagement, and content pipeline status. Built with Class-Based Views, PostgreSQL aggregation, and a JSON API for D3 chart rendering.

Django CBVs PostgreSQL Aggregation D3.js charts Celery Redis cache
PostgreSQL
→
Django ORM
annotate / aggregate
→
JSON API
DRF endpoints
→
D3.js
charts & maps

ORM Aggregation Pipeline

Uses TruncMonth, Count, Avg, and Sum to compute article volume, average word count, and revenue by month directly in the database.

Real-Time Content Pipeline

Custom model managers track articles through stages: pitch, draft, editing, published. Dashboard shows bottlenecks with a Kanban-style board view.

Author Leaderboard

Annotates authors with article count, total views, and engagement rate. Uses select_related and prefetch_related to avoid N+1 queries.

Cached Computation Layer

Expensive dashboard queries are cached in Redis with automatic invalidation via Django signals. Cache keys include the date range for granular control.

How I Built This

  • Class-Based Views with LoginRequiredMixin and custom get_context_data() to inject aggregated statistics
  • PostgreSQL-specific features: TruncMonth, ArrayAgg, and JSONBAgg for complex reporting queries
  • DRF serializers with SerializerMethodField for computed values like engagement rate and trending score
  • Celery beat tasks that pre-compute daily/weekly/monthly rollups and store them in a DashboardSnapshot model
  • Template inheritance: base dashboard layout with overridable content blocks for each section
  • Custom management command import_analytics that parses CSV exports from Google Analytics
Project 02

Public Records API

A RESTful API that serves government public records (FOIA requests, campaign finance, voting records) with full-text search, filtering, pagination, and rate limiting. Designed for journalists and civic tech developers.

Django REST Framework Full-Text Search FilterSets Throttling OpenAPI docs JWT auth
Gov Data
CSV / XML feeds
→
Django
import commands
→
PostgreSQL
full-text index
→
REST API
/api/v1/records/

Full-Text Search

PostgreSQL SearchVector and SearchRank for weighted search across document titles, descriptions, and content. GIN indexes for fast queries.

Advanced Filtering

DRF FilterSets with django-filter for date ranges, category filtering, geographic lookups, and full-text search combined in a single query.

API Rate Limiting

Custom throttle classes: 100 requests/hour for anonymous users, 1000/hour for authenticated. API keys issued via the Django admin.

Data Import Pipeline

Management commands that fetch, parse, and normalize government data from CSV, XML, and JSON feeds. Idempotent imports with checksum deduplication.

How I Built This

  • ViewSets with ModelViewSet for CRUD and ReadOnlyModelViewSet for public read-only endpoints
  • Custom SearchFilter backend combining SearchVector weights (title=A, body=B, tags=C) for relevance ranking
  • CursorPagination for stable pagination on large datasets, avoiding the offset-based performance cliff
  • JWT authentication via djangorestframework-simplejwt with token refresh and blacklisting
  • Auto-generated OpenAPI schema with drf-spectacular for interactive API docs at /api/docs/
  • Custom management command with --dry-run flag for safe data imports and --since for incremental updates
Project 03

Election Results Tracker

A live election results dashboard modeled after broadcast TV election night graphics. Ingests real-time AP election data, processes results by precinct, and serves a real-time WebSocket feed for live updating displays.

Django Channels WebSocket Celery Real-time PostgreSQL D3.js maps
AP Feed
election data
→
Celery
ingest worker
→
PostgreSQL
results store
→
WebSocket
live updates

Real-Time Data Pipeline

Celery tasks poll the AP election feed every 30 seconds, parse XML results, and upsert into PostgreSQL with update_or_create() for idempotent processing.

WebSocket Broadcasting

Django Channels consumers broadcast updated race results to all connected clients. Channel layers use Redis for cross-process message routing.

Race Call Logic

Custom model methods implement race projection logic: margin thresholds, precincts reporting percentage, and manual override for edge cases.

Geographic Results

County-level results stored with FIPS codes for joining with GeoJSON boundaries. API endpoints serve data for D3 choropleth maps.

How I Built This

  • Django Channels with AsyncWebsocketConsumer for broadcasting race updates to connected newsroom displays
  • Celery beat scheduler runs ingest_ap_results every 30 seconds during active election periods
  • Race model with @property methods for computed fields: margin, leader, precincts_pct, is_called
  • Django signals on RaceResult.post_save trigger WebSocket broadcasts via the channel layer
  • Admin interface with custom actions: "Call Race", "Retract Call", "Mark Too Close" for editorial control
  • Test suite with factory-generated election data simulating different race scenarios (blowout, toss-up, recount)
Project 04

Investigative Story Manager

A secure document management and collaboration platform for investigative journalism teams. Handles document uploads, source management, timeline construction, and secure sharing with end-to-end encryption for sensitive materials.

Custom User Model File Uploads Permissions Audit Logging Encryption Multi-tenant
Documents
encrypted upload
→
Django
access control
→
S3 Storage
encrypted at rest
→
Audit Log
every access

Role-Based Access Control

Custom permissions model: reporters, editors, and legal review. Object-level permissions with django-guardian for per-document access control.

Secure Document Pipeline

Files uploaded via chunked upload, encrypted with AES-256, stored in S3 with server-side encryption. Download links expire after configurable time windows.

Investigation Timeline

A TimelineEntry model tracks key events, document discoveries, and source contacts. Rendered as an interactive timeline with D3.

Comprehensive Audit Trail

Custom middleware logs every document view, download, and share. AuditLog model records user, action, IP address, and timestamp for legal compliance.

How I Built This

  • Custom AbstractUser subclass with role field, two-factor auth via django-otp, and mandatory password rotation
  • Object-level permissions using django-guardian: editors can share, reporters can view, legal can approve for publication
  • Custom Storage backend that encrypts files before upload to S3 and decrypts on download with per-document keys
  • Audit middleware captures request.user, request.path, request.method, and REMOTE_ADDR for every request
  • Investigation model with ManyToManyField to sources, documents, and team members with a through model for access timestamps
  • Management command generate_audit_report exports activity logs as CSV for legal review and FOIA compliance
